signals.reasoning: Existing ETH long remains a small bounded-risk management hold with live exchange protection already in place, so this is still not an urgent cleanup exit. Even with the board under VETO, the risk on the open ETH position is already defined and there is no cleaner reason to force a close.

BTC is still digesting the prior squeeze and the live headlines are keeping the full board under VETO. That leaves it neutral from a position-management perspective rather than a reason to rotate away from ETH.
SOL is moving with the same broad crypto tape and does not present a cleaner management alternative than the current ETH position. Keep it watch-only under the active event brake.
TST remains a noisy momentum name after a sharp squeeze move, which keeps fresh action low quality under VETO. Better to avoid adding complexity while managing ETH.
STBL can still look mechanically interesting, but it remains too unstable and event-sensitive to outrank the existing ETH hold. Watch-only on this board.
FOGO is a thin high-beta name where execution quality can deteriorate quickly under headline-driven conditions. Not a cleaner management rotation than the current bounded-risk ETH long.
PEOPLE looks more like rebound noise than a clean fresh trend. Position-management priority plus VETO keeps it in the hold bucket.
MERL is still in rebound mode without becoming a clearly superior opportunity. It does not justify rotating away from the open ETH position.
WLD is notable, but it remains too reactive and too dependent on tape behavior to trust as a cleaner alternative under VETO. Keep it watch-only.
LDO still has some constructive rebound structure, but not enough separation to justify a switch away from ETH. Under VETO it remains a hold-only watchlist name.
GRIFFAIN is weak enough to notice but already messy after the drop, not a clean fresh short. Position management first means avoid forcing a second idea while ETH is already on the book.
